$schema: Agent team role definitions shared by Codex and Claude agent teams
description: Role definitions shared across Codex and Claude agent teams for frontend-dev and backend-dev. Projects may still create local overrides in platform team-config directories when a repo needs extra specialization.

architecture: Follow FSD architecture (entities > features > widgets > pages)
Use shadcn/ui + Tailwind patterns for UI surfaces
Consume backend contracts without adding local normalizers or transform fallbacks
testing: Prefer Vitest and React Testing Library for unit/component coverage
Use MSW when API mocking is required
Preserve existing frontend test conventions and nearby patterns

architecture: Follow canonical backend specs in docs/specs/ as the source of truth
Keep route -> controller -> service -> model/DTO boundaries clean
Backend owns contracts: no backward-compat layers, silent transforms, or normalizer fallbacks
testing: Prefer the repo's backend unit/integration test patterns
Validate canonical response envelopes and DTO alignment when routes change
Preserve targeted coverage for service, controller, and contract behavior

responsibilities: Break issue into parallelizable tasks with dependencies
Spawn and coordinate teammates
Review completed work against code-reviewer-config.json criteria
Run Phase 3 (Dev Verification) checks as gate
Execute Phase 3 documentation (session file, resolution notes)
Execute Phase 6 (Comms Response) if cross-team
Synthesize teammate results and resolve conflicts
Manage dev_communication messaging and issue updates
doesNotOwn: Phase 4 (QA Verification) — owned by QA team or QA sub-team
Phase 5 (Completion) — only QA moves issues to completed/

responsibilities: Implement source code following the active dev sub-role architecture
Create or update TypeScript types in the appropriate domain layer
Follow patterns from memory/ and applicable ADRs
Use existing abstractions and conventions
Maintain contract alignment for the active team surface
implementer.spawnPrompt: You are an implementer teammate for the active dev sub-role. Your task: {task_description}. Start by reading team.json and ai_team_config/roles/{sub-role}.yaml to confirm whether the active role is frontend-dev or backend-dev. If frontend-dev, follow FSD architecture (entities > features > widgets > pages), use shadcn/ui + Tailwind when working in UI surfaces, and consume backend contracts without adding normalizers. If backend-dev, follow the canonical backend specs in docs/specs/, keep route/controller/service/model + DTO boundaries clean, and preserve backend contract ownership with no backward-compat layers or silent transforms. Use existing patterns from memory/patterns/ and ADRs. Do NOT write tests - the tester teammate handles that. Ensure TypeScript compiles cleanly.

tester.spawnWhen: Issue requires new tests or has test acceptance criteria
responsibilities: Write tests using the active dev sub-role's test stack
Write integration tests for complex flows when the repo supports them
Ensure tests have meaningful assertions
Cover edge cases (empty states, errors, loading)
Create focused mocks and fixtures that match the active team's conventions
tester.spawnPrompt: You are a tester teammate for the active dev sub-role. Your task: {task_description}. Start by reading team.json and ai_team_config/roles/{sub-role}.yaml. If frontend-dev, write tests with Vitest, React Testing Library, and MSW when API mocking is required. If backend-dev, write tests with the repo's backend unit/integration patterns and validate canonical response, contract, and service behavior when API surfaces change. Tests must have meaningful assertions, not just smoke renders. Cover happy path, error states, loading or failure paths where relevant, and edge cases. Follow testing patterns in memory/patterns/ and check nearby test files for conventions.

researcher.spawnWhen: Issue is complex and requires deep codebase understanding before implementation
responsibilities: Analyze existing code patterns relevant to the issue
Find similar implementations to use as reference
Identify affected files and potential impacts
Map architecture and layer dependencies for the change
Report findings to lead for task planning
researcher.spawnPrompt: You are a researcher teammate. Investigate the codebase to support: {task_description}. Find existing patterns, similar implementations, affected files, and potential impacts. Check memory/patterns/ and memory/entities/ for documented conventions. Report your findings clearly with file paths and code references. Do NOT write or modify any code.

solo.description: Single agent, no team. Default for simple issues.

paired.description: Lead + implementer. For focused implementation work.
selectWhen: Issue touches 2-4 files in the same architectural layer or domain slice
Tests already exist and just need updating

standard.description: Lead + implementer + tester. Default team mode.
selectWhen: Issue requires new code AND new tests
Feature spans 3+ files across architectural layers

research.description: Lead + researcher + implementer + tester. For complex unknowns.
selectWhen: Issue involves unfamiliar parts of codebase
No existing patterns in memory/ match the task

parallel-impl.description: Lead + 2 implementers + tester. For multi-component features.
selectWhen: Changes span distinct architectural layers or domains that can be split safely
Components can be built in parallel without file conflicts
Large feature with natural component boundaries

teamSelection.phase: Phase 1.5 - runs after context loading, before implementation
steps: Read issue requirements and acceptance criteria
Count files likely to be created/modified
Check if patterns exist in memory/ for this type of work
Assess complexity: simple (1-2 files), moderate (3-5 files), complex (6+ files)
Check if issue has cross-team dependencies
Select best preset (default: standard for team mode, solo for normal mode)

overrides.userExplicit: User can specify preset name when spawning teams
overrides.leadJudgment: Lead can override preset during Phase 2T review checkpoint

midDevReview.phase: Phase 2T checkpoint - runs after 50% of tasks complete
triggers: Half of team tasks marked complete
Any teammate blocked by hooks 2+ times consecutively
Teammate sends message indicating scope change
actions: Assess progress: are tasks completing on schedule?
Check for scope creep: has the issue grown beyond original plan?
Evaluate teammate effectiveness: any teammate idle too long or struggling?
Decide: add teammate, remove teammate, reassign tasks, or continue as-is
If adding: spawn new teammate with remaining task context
If removing: shut down underperforming teammate, reassign their tasks
If scope changed: create new tasks, adjust dependencies

postDevReview.phase: Phase 3 (Dev Verification) - runs during documentation, after implementation complete
postDevReview.purpose: Review team config effectiveness and record adjustments for future similar issues
steps: Compare actual team composition to selected preset
Record which teammates were most/least effective
Note if mid-dev review changed the team composition
Identify what preset would have been ideal in hindsight
Check: did the task dependency pattern match the actual flow?
Record timing: how long did each teammate's tasks take?
Write team review to session file under ## Team Review section
If effectiveness was excellent/good, promote to memory/team-configs/ using _template.json

learnedConfigStorage.purpose: Structured lookup for Phase 1.5 team selection, avoids parsing session prose

fields: Mid-dev adjustments: {none | added X | removed X | reassigned Y}
Recommendation: {use same preset | try different preset} for similar issues

bugFix.description: Bug fix with regression test (TDD style)

coordinationRules.fileOwnership: Each teammate owns distinct files. No two teammates edit the same file.
coordinationRules.communicationPattern: Teammates report to lead via task updates. Lead broadcasts only for scope changes.
coordinationRules.conflictResolution: Lead resolves conflicts. If teammates disagree on approach, lead decides.
coordinationRules.modelOverride: Lead can specify different models per teammate if needed.
coordinationRules.shutdownOrder: Shut down all teammates before Phase 3 documentation. Lead handles Phase 3 (Dev Verification + documentation) and Phase 6 (Comms Response) solo. Phases 4-5 are owned by QA.
